mechanism: Broad tyrosine kinase inhibitor with activity against FLT3 and other kinases
additional_notes: Has preclinical activity in FLT3 mutant AML models. No explicit concentrations provided in source.

mechanism: Inhibits BCL‑2 family proteins (BCL‑2, BCL‑XL), promoting apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑263; useful to probe apoptotic dependency. No explicit concentrations provided in source.

mechanism: Inhibits CDKs including CDK9, downregulates MCL‑1 and induces apoptosis
additional_notes: Also called flavopiridol; used to lower MCL‑1 in AML models. No explicit concentrations provided in source.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple cyclin‑dependent kinases causing cytotoxicity and potential MCL‑1 reduction
additional_notes: Reported cytotoxicity in AML lines; may synergize with BCL‑2 inhibitors. No explicit concentrations provided in source.

mechanism: Selective CDK4/6 inhibition leading to cell‑cycle arrest
additional_notes: Can block cell‑cycle progression and sensitize AML cells to other agents in some contexts. No explicit concentrations provided in source.

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 in the MAPK pathway, reducing MAPK signaling
additional_notes: MAPK pathway inhibition can be effective alone or combined with FLT3 inhibitors. No explicit concentrations provided in source.

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases broadly, altering chromatin and gene expression to reduce proliferation
additional_notes: Demonstrated antiproliferative effects in AML cell lines and combination regimens. No explicit concentrations provided in source.

mechanism: Class I HDAC inhibition leading to epigenetic modulation and potential synergy with hypomethylating agents
additional_notes: Shown synergy with hypomethylating agents in AML models. No explicit concentrations provided in source.

mechanism: Inhibits the 26S proteasome, leading to accumulation of misfolded proteins and apoptosis
additional_notes: Tested clinically in AML combinations; reduces viability in AML models. No explicit concentrations provided in source.

mechanism: Irreversible proteasome inhibitor causing proteotoxic stress and cell death
additional_notes: Second‑generation proteasome inhibitor with potent cytotoxicity in hematologic models. No explicit concentrations provided in source.

mechanism: Inhibits mTOR signaling, affecting cell growth and metabolism
additional_notes: Rapamycin mentioned as related mTOR inhibitor; mTOR pathway relevant to AML growth. No explicit concentrations provided in source.

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase, increases ROS and induces ROS‑dependent cell death
additional_notes: Repurposed anti‑rheumatic with preclinical cytotoxicity in AML. No explicit concentrations provided in source.

mechanism: Reported ALDH‑related and proteasome pathway effects; can induce apoptosis, often studied with copper
additional_notes: Mentioned in combination with copper in literature; induces apoptosis in cancer models. No explicit concentrations provided in source.

mechanism: Binds tubulin and disrupts microtubules, impairing cell division
additional_notes: Anti‑helminth with reported antitumor activity in leukemia models in vitro and in vivo. No explicit concentrations provided in source.

mechanism: Mitochondrial uncoupler and modulates pathways such as Wnt/STAT, leading to anticancer effects
additional_notes: Reported preclinical anticancer and anti‑leukemia activity; repurposing candidate. No explicit concentrations provided in source.
